# Performance Checklist: Web Framework Research Spike

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-004's graph-rendering, performance-note, reproduction, and package-footprint requirements are complete, measurable, and sufficient for later web specs.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the 1k-node graph-rendering target requirements complete enough to require node count, edge count, reached-or-fallback status, measured observations, and downstream limitations? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Spec §SC-004, Plan §Prototype Method]
- [x] CHK002 Are performance-note requirements complete for first visible render timing, interaction observations, machine/browser context, dataset size, and limitations? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §Prototype Method]
- [x] CHK003 Are candidate package-footprint requirements complete for both framework candidates and the selected graph-rendering library or approach? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Hard Gates]
- [x] CHK004 Are reproduction-step requirements complete enough to cover clean checkout setup, local data generation, prototype evidence recreation, screenshot capture, and fallback recording? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Spec §SC-005, Quickstart §4, Quickstart §5]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is "1k-node target" defined with enough precision to distinguish the required target from representative self-repo data and closest-achieved fallback evidence? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Prototype Method]
- [x] CHK006 Is "acceptable package footprint" tied to measured compressed or uncompressed size evidence and a decision-document threshold rather than an undefined judgment call? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Hard Gates]
- [x] CHK007 Are graph interaction observations categorized clearly enough for later SPEC-006 planning, including pan/zoom, node focus or selection, details on demand, traversal, visible status, legend, and search affordances? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Clarity, Plan §Weighted Scoring Model, Plan §Prototype Method]
- [x] CHK008 Are screenshot captions required to state the dataset, node and edge counts, capture tool, image dimensions, and graph details visible to reviewers? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Clarity, Spec §FR-011, Spec §SC-004]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK009 Can maintainers objectively assess whether the graph-rendering evidence is sufficient to unblock SPEC-006 without rerunning framework selection? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-007, Plan §Artifact Layout]
- [x] CHK010 Can maintainers objectively reproduce the performance evidence from documented commands and local assets alone? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Spec §SC-005, Quickstart §1, Quickstart §5]
- [x] CHK011 Can hard-gate footprint decisions be objectively reviewed from recorded package metadata, production asset sizes, runtime dependency size, and rationale? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Data Model §PackageMetadata, Data Model §HardGateResult]
- [x] CHK012 Can the selected graph-rendering library or approach be evaluated against offline, permissive-license, package-shipping, and footprint constraints before recommendation? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Spec §FR-016, Plan §Dependency policy]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK013 Are requirements defined for the full success path where representative self-repo data and the 1k-node target both produce screenshot and performance evidence? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Coverage, Spec §US2, Plan §Self-Repo UAT]
- [x] CHK014 Are requirements defined for the fallback path where the 1k-node target is missed, including blocker, closest achieved result, and downstream implication? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-017]
- [x] CHK015 Are requirements defined for unavailable browser tooling or screenshot capture fallback while preserving evidence quality for reviewers? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Coverage, Spec §FR-017, Plan §Prototype Method]
- [x] CHK016 Are requirements defined for package metadata that is unavailable, stale, conflicting, or insufficient for footprint and maintenance decisions? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Research §Current-Source Research Procedure]

## SPEC-006 Readiness

- [x] CHK017 Are deferred graph-browser concerns mapped to SPEC-006 with enough detail for later scope decisions around overview, zoom/filter, details on demand, relationship tracing, and local route or state behavior? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Traceability, Spec §SC-007, Plan §Weighted Scoring Model]
- [x] CHK018 Are graph-rendering limitations required to distinguish selected-stack limitations from graph-rendering-library limitations and dataset-generation limitations? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Clarity, Data Model §GraphPrototypeEvidence, Contract §Evidence Requirements]

## Re-run Assessment

- [x] CHK019 Are the 1k-node target, representative self-repo dataset, closest-achieved fallback, and blocker requirements still aligned across spec, plan, quickstart, and data model? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Prototype Method, Quickstart §4, Data Model §GraphPrototypeEvidence]
- [x] CHK020 Are performance-note requirements still sufficient to support SPEC-006 planning without requiring production web code in SPEC-004? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Plan §Performance Goals, Spec §FR-015, Contract §Forbidden Durable Changes]
- [x] CHK021 Are package-footprint requirements still reviewable across current metadata, measured production asset size, runtime dependency size, and decision-document rationale? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Hard Gates, Data Model §PackageMetadata]
- [x] CHK022 Are screenshot evidence requirements still measurable through committed PNG paths, capture metadata, image dimensions, readable graph details, and fallback handling? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Spec §FR-011, Spec §SC-004, Contract §Screenshot Assets]
- [x] CHK023 Are SPEC-006 downstream concerns still required to be mapped explicitly rather than implied by the graph prototype evidence? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Traceability, Spec §SC-007, Contract §Decision Artifacts]
